  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Sharpening the contribution claim and gap statement is a primary candidate for what 'stronger' means in an academic abstract — the current summary paragraph is underassertive about significance and novelty.",
    "R1.2": "Logical flow from motivation through methodology to conclusion is central to argumentative strength; the causal chain from confounder identification to debiasing to ranking reordering needs tightening.",
    "R1.6": "'Stronger' in academic prose standardly implicates sentence-level impact, precision, and rhetorical force — passive constructions and hedged phrasing are present throughout."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Does 'stronger' target primarily (a) prose impact and sentence-level clarity (making R1.6 load-bearing), or (b) the argumentative case — sharpening the contribution claim and logical chain (making R1.1 and R1.2 load-bearing)? These demand materially different interventions."
}

rationale.R1.1: Sharpening the contribution claim and gap statement is a primary candidate for what 'stronger' means in an academic abstract — the current summary paragraph is underassertive about significance and novelty.
rationale.R1.2: Logical flow from motivation through methodology to conclusion is central to argumentative strength; the causal chain from confounder identification to debiasing to ranking reordering needs tightening.
rationale.R1.6: 'Stronger' in academic prose standardly implicates sentence-level impact, precision, and rhetorical force — passive constructions and hedged phrasing are present throughout.

parsed.clarification_question: Does 'stronger' target primarily (a) prose impact and sentence-level clarity (making R1.6 load-bearing), or (b) the argumentative case — sharpening the contribution claim and logical chain (making R1.1 and R1.2 load-bearing)? These demand materially different interventions.
